reasoning: All four correctly center the unobserved counterfactual and garden-of-forking-paths and propose the right evidence hierarchy (diff-in-diff, synthetic control, RDD, pre-registration, mechanism/placebo). A edges ahead with the sharpest non-obvious insight (camps answered different estimands so both can be partly correct), a clean evidence ranking ordered by counterfactual constraint, and a pre-committed falsifiable prediction test. C is nearly equal, naming the full menu of rival causes (pre-trend, confounder, reverse causation, regression to mean) and the best operational closing line. B adds the incommensurability point and the multiverse but is slightly diffuse. D is competent with a strong wage-subsidy example but most compressed and adds least structure on bias mechanics.
